generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance MotorActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Permanent magnet synchronous motor driving the first vertical axis of MABI MAX 100 industrial robot"
]

instance GearboxActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Cycloidal gearbox component of the first axis transmission system"
]

instance CycloidalGearboxActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Primary cycloidal gearbox component for high-precision motion transmission"
]

// C3: Physical sensing components
instance MotorEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder attached to the motor shaft measuring angular position, velocity, and torque via manufacturer-specific dynamics models"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorPositionData, MotorVelocityData, MotorTorqueData
]

instance SecondaryEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Secondary encoder measuring angular position and output angle of the gearbox system"
    DTDFVocab:producedData GearboxPositionData, OutputAngleData
]

instance MotorCurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Current sensor measuring motor input current (_I_M) via manufacturer-specific dynamics models"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorCurrentData
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance MotorPositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorEncoder
]

instance MotorVelocityData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorEncoder
]

instance MotorTorqueData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorEncoder
]

instance GearboxPositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SecondaryEncoder
]

instance OutputAngleData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SecondaryEncoder
]

instance MotorCurrentData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorCurrentSensor
]

instance MeasuredMotorCurrent : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time measured motor current (_I_M) streamed via MQTT protocol"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorCurrentData
]

instance MeasuredMotorTorque : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Measured motor torque (_τ_M) derived from current measurements"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorTorqueData
]

instance MeasuredMotorVelocity : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Measured motor angular velocity (ω_M) from encoder data"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorVelocityData
]

instance MeasuredGearboxTorque : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Measured gearbox torque (τ_G) derived from encoder differences accounting for elasticity"
    DTDFVocab:fromData GearsTorqueStream
]

instance MeasuredOutputAngle : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Measured output angle from secondary encoder measurements"
    DTDFVocab:fromData OutputAngleData
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance IdealSystemModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MathWorks MATLAB Simulink model representing the ideal system (Fig. 4a) without friction effects"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MATLABSimulink
]

instance CoulombViscousFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulink model implementing Coulomb-Viscous friction effects for motor-gearbox system"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MATLABSimulink
]

instance StribeckFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulink model implementing Stribeck friction model to simulate velocity-dependent friction"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MATLABSimulink
]

instance DahlFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulink model implementing Dahl friction model for complex hysteresis effects"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MATLABSimulink
]

instance LuGreFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulink model implementing LuGre friction model combining Coulomb and viscous components with velocity-dependent stiffness"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MATLABSimulink
]

instance ReferenceOutputData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Reference output signals (_r_M, _r_G, _r_out) for motor, gearbox, and system outputs"
    DTDFVocab:fromData ReferenceOutputStream
]

instance FrictionCompensatedOutputData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Friction-compensated outputs (δ_r_M, δ_r_G, δ_r_out) for comparison with live measurements"
    DTDFVocab:fromData FrictionCompensationStream
]

instance ReferenceOutputStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ReferenceOutputData
]

instance FrictionCompensationStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FrictionCompensatedOutputData
]

instance GearsTorqueStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SecondaryEncoder
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RealTimeFrictionIdentification
]

instance StructuralSysMLModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Block Definition Diagram and Internal Block Diagram representing first axis components (Motor, Gearbox, Cycloidal Gearbox) and their interconnections"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLTool
]

instance AssetAdministrationShell : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "AAS framework integrating digital shadow data with Siemens SINUMERIK 840D CNC controller"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AASTool
]

// C11: Enablers
instance MATLABSimulink : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks MATLAB Simulink platform for executable friction models and system dynamics simulation"
    DTDFVocab:enables IdealSystemModel, CoulombViscousFrictionModel, StribeckFrictionModel, DahlFrictionModel, LuGreFrictionModel
]

instance SysMLTool : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML modeling tool for creating Block Definition Diagrams and Internal Block Diagrams of the structural model"
    DTDFVocab:enables StructuralSysMLModel
]

instance MQTTProtocol : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT protocol for real-time sensor data streaming (motor current, angular position, velocity, torque)"
    DTDFVocab:enables MotorCurrentData, MotorPositionData, MotorVelocityData, GearboxPositionData, OutputAngleData, GearsTorqueStream
]

instance AASTool : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Asset Administration Shell framework for integrating digital shadow with Siemens SINUMERIK 840D CNC controller"
    DTDFVocab:enables AssetAdministrationShell
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeFrictionIdentification : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous, multi-physics-aware identification of white-box (analytical) and black-box (data-driven) friction models using live sensor data"
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionDetectionInsight, ModelParameterUpdateAction
]

instance ModelBasedControlOptimization : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Integration of digital shadow data into Asset Administration Shells for enhanced motion accuracy via improved friction compensation"
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlParameterOptimizationAction
]

instance OperationalMonitoring : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Visualization of friction trends (wear, tool changes) enabling proactive adjustments"
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionTrendInsight
]

instance DataAggregation : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Aggregation of sensor traces (motor and gearbox outputs) into DataTraces in the digital shadow"
    DTDFVocab:provides TraceabilityAction
]

instance ReferenceVsSimulatedComparison : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Unified visualization of reference vs. simulated data traces with friction model outputs for deviation detection"
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualizationInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance FrictionDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time identification of static, kinetic, and viscous friction via discrepancies between measured and simulated data"
]

instance ModelParameterUpdateAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic update of friction model parameters during operation based on sensor discrepancies"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ControlParameterOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Application of optimized control parameters derived from friction models to improve motion accuracy"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance FrictionTrendInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Visualization of friction trends (wear patterns, tool changes) enabling proactive maintenance decisions"
]

instance TraceabilityAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Ensuring all data traces originate from physical sources (encoders) or computational models (Simulink) with transitive traceability"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance VisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Comparison visualization between reference outputs, simulated outputs, and friction-compensated outputs for control tuning"
]

// C12: Data Streams
instance MotorDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorEncoder, MotorCurrentSensor
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RealTimeFrictionIdentification, ModelBasedControlOptimization
]

instance GearboxDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SecondaryEncoder
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RealTimeFrictionIdentification, ModelBasedControlOptimization
]

instance Environment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains MotorActuator, GearboxActuator, CycloidalGearboxActuator,
                 MotorEncoder, SecondaryEncoder, MotorCurrentSensor,
                 IdealSystemModel, CoulombViscousFrictionModel, StribeckFrictionModel,
                 DahlFrictionModel, LuGreFrictionModel, AssetAdministrationShell
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The twinning time scale is **real-time (low-latency)** with:
  - **Data acquisition frequency**: Sensor data (e.g., motor current, torque, angular velocity) is streamed in **real-time** via MQTT, enabling **sub-second updates** for friction modeling.
  - **Model recalibration frequency**: Continuous, **online identification** of friction models during operation, with adjustments based on live data traces (e.g., sensor discrepancies between measured and simulated outputs)."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The digital twin constellation is **centralized for the first axis** but modular for scalability:
  - **Core components**:
    - **Digital Shadow (DS)**: A **passive, one-way data flow** (Kritzinger et al. reference model) linking physical sensor data to simulation models (e.g., friction models).
    - **Processing component**: Executes simulation models (e.g., MathWorks Simulink) to generate data traces (e.g., ˆ_r_M, ˆ_r_G) for comparison with live measurements.
  - **Integration with industrial systems**:
    - **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)**: Enables integration with Siemens SINUMERIK 840D CNC controller for real-time control adjustments.
    - **Horizontal integration**: Future extension to other robot axes via standardized data traces and modular DS components."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The digital twin covers the **operational lifecycle stage** of the robot, focusing on:
  - **As-operated phase**: Real-time friction analysis during **actual machining operations** to account for wear, tool changes, and environmental factors (e.g., temperature, load).
  - **No explicit design or manufacturing stages**: The digital shadow is **not used for initial robot design or manufacturing validation**, but rather for **continuous improvement** of motion accuracy during operation."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Fidelity and validity considerations include:
  - **Model fidelity**:
    - **White-box models**: Analytical friction models (e.g., Coulomb-Viscous) account for load, temperature, and time-dependent effects.
    - **Black-box models**: Data-driven friction models (e.g., LuGre) reduce parameter identification needs but require continuous recalibration.
  - **Validation mechanisms**:
    - **Live-data comparison**: Reference vs. simulated data traces (e.g., _r_M vs. ˆ_r_M) to detect friction-induced inaccuracies.
    - **Uncertainty handling**: Encoder differences account for **elasticity and backlash** in the gearbox.
  - **Verification**: SysML models (e.g., Fig. 3) ensure structural consistency, while Simulink models validate friction model outputs."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The digital twin technical connection uses:
  - **Data transmission**: MQTT protocol for real-time sensor data streaming (e.g., motor current, torque, angular velocity).
  - **Model integration**: MathWorks MATLAB Simulink for executable friction models linked to physical sensor data.
  - **Industrial interface**: Asset Administration Shell (AAS) for integration with Siemens SINUMERIK 840D CNC controller."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The digital twin is deployed via:
  - **Dashboard Visualization**: A **local or cloud-hosted** platform (specific deployment location not specified) displays aggregated data traces and friction analysis results in real-time.
  - **MQTT Broker**: Likely deployed on a **local server** or edge device to manage real-time sensor data streams and processing workflows."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "Horizontal integration is **not explicitly detailed** for this single-axis DS, but the methodology supports:
  - **Standardized data traces** (e.g., motor current, torque) across axes.
  - **Modular DS components** (e.g., SysML models, Simulink simulations) for future extension to other robot axes."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The digital twin adheres to:
  - **Digital Shadow Reference Model (DSRM)**: UML-based structure (Fig. 1) for traceability and metadata (e.g., purpose, asset, data sources).
  - **SysML standards**: For structural modeling (e.g., Block Definition Diagrams, Internal Block Diagrams).
  - **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)**: Industry-standard for integrating digital twins with industrial systems (e.g., Siemens SINUMERIK 840D)."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The digital twin addresses:
  - **Data Security**: While the document mentions **MQTT** for sensor data transmission, it does not specify encryption or authentication mechanisms. The **Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl** controller likely enforces security protocols for robot communication.
  - **Physical Safety**: The robot’s **safety cell** restricts the working range to **-90° to +90°**, and the **maximum speed** is capped at **75°/s** to prevent unsafe operations. The digital twin does not generate commands to the physical system, mitigating remote operation risks.
  - **Limitation**: The document does not detail cybersecurity measures (e.g., TLS for MQTT, access controls) or safety validation protocols for the digital twin."
]
